senses.definition: Plural of artfulness: clever and skillful ways of doing something, often to achieve a goal.
examples: The novel is full of stylistic artfulnesses that reward careful reading.
She admired the many artfulnesses in his argument, even if she disagreed.
The director’s different artfulnesses make each film feel unique.

senses.definition: A soft, crumbly eraser used by artists to remove pencil or charcoal marks.
examples: She used an artgum to lighten the shading on the portrait.
Keep an artgum in your sketch kit for gentle erasing.
The art teacher recommended an artgum for charcoal drawings.

senses.definition: In Indian philosophy, material prosperity, success, and practical goals in life.
examples: In Hindu thought, artha is one of the four aims of human life.
He pursued artha through his business career while trying to remain ethical.
The text explains how artha should be balanced with duty and spiritual growth.

senses.definition: A medical image or X‑ray of a joint taken after a contrast dye is injected.
examples: The doctor ordered an arthrogram to examine the damaged shoulder.
An arthrogram can show soft tissue problems that a standard X‑ray might miss.
You may feel some pressure when the contrast is injected before the arthrogram.

senses.definition: Describing films that are artistic, experimental, or made for a small, serious audience rather than for mass entertainment.
examples: They prefer arthouse films to big Hollywood blockbusters.
The festival focuses on arthouse productions from around the world.
He directed an arthouse drama that impressed critics.

senses.definition: A cinema that mainly shows serious, artistic, or foreign films.
examples: We watched a French movie at the local arthouse.
The arthouse downtown is known for its film retrospectives.
Her documentary screened at several arthouses across Europe.

senses.definition: Plural of arthouse: cinemas that mainly show serious, artistic, or foreign films.
examples: Her film was released in arthouses across the country.
Many arthouses struggle to compete with large multiplexes.
European arthouses often host director Q&A sessions.

senses.definition: Plural of arthron: joints or jointed segments in animals, especially invertebrates.
examples: The arthra of the insect’s legs allow complex movement.
Under the microscope, the arthra are clearly separated by thin membranes.
Researchers counted the arthra along the crustacean’s body.

senses.definition: A historical term for severe joint pain, especially in gout.
examples: The old texts describe arthragra as a painful swelling of the joints.
He was said to suffer from arthragra in his hands and feet.
Treatments for arthragra were limited in earlier centuries.

senses.definition: Relating to a joint of the body.
examples: The patient complained of arthral pain after the injury.
Arthral tissues showed signs of inflammation.
These drugs target arthral structures to reduce discomfort.

senses.definition: Pain in one or more joints, without saying what is causing it.
examples: The main symptom was arthralgia in the knees and ankles.
Patients with viral infections often report arthralgia.
Persistent arthralgia should be examined by a specialist.

senses.definition: Plural of arthralgia: pains in one or more joints.
examples: The disease is marked by fevers and multiple arthralgias.
She described migrating arthralgias affecting different joints each week.
These medications can sometimes cause arthralgias as side effects.

senses.definition: Related to or involving joint pain.
examples: Arthralgic symptoms appeared two days after the infection.
She takes medication to manage her arthralgic complaints.
The study compared arthralgic patients with healthy controls.

senses.definition: Plural of arthrectomy: surgical operations to remove tissue from a joint.
examples: The surgeon has performed several arthrectomies this year.
Arthrectomies may relieve pain when conservative treatments fail.
The outcomes of arthrectomies vary depending on the joint involved.

senses.definition: Surgical removal of damaged or diseased tissue from a joint.
examples: An arthrectomy was recommended to reduce the patient’s knee pain.
After the arthrectomy, she began physical therapy within a week.
The surgeon explained the risks of arthrectomy before the procedure.

senses.definition: Swelling of a joint due to fluid buildup.
examples: Arthredema was visible in the patient’s ankle after the sprain.
The doctor noted arthredema and warmth around the joint.
Persistent arthredema may indicate underlying joint disease.

senses.definition: The formation of pus in a joint, usually due to infection.
examples: Arthrempyesis requires urgent treatment to prevent joint damage.
The patient was diagnosed with arthrempyesis of the knee.
Untreated arthrempyesis can lead to severe complications.

senses.definition: The sense of movement and position in a joint.
examples: Impaired arthresthesia can affect balance and coordination.
Tests of arthresthesia help evaluate nerve function.
The injury led to reduced arthresthesia in the wrist.

senses.definition: Having arthritis or showing signs of arthritis.
examples: She walks slowly because of her arthritic knees.
Arthritic joints can become stiff in cold weather.
The dog needed medicine for its arthritic hips.

senses.definition: A person who has arthritis.
examples: As an arthritic, he finds it hard to climb stairs.
The clinic offers support groups for arthritics.
Many arthritics benefit from gentle exercise.

senses.definition: Relating to or affected by arthritis.
examples: Arthritical changes were visible on the X‑ray.
She has long suffered from arthritical problems in her fingers.
The report described arthritical deformities in the joints.

senses.definition: In a way that is affected by arthritis or resembles arthritis.
examples: He moved arthritically across the room on cold mornings.
Her fingers curled arthritically around the pen.
The old dog walked arthritically after long naps.

senses.definition: Having the nature of or relating to arthritis.
examples: Arthriticine changes were found in the spinal joints.
The sample showed arthriticine features under the microscope.
Arthriticine conditions can worsen with age.

senses.definition: Plural of arthritic: people who have arthritis.
examples: The clinic offers special exercise classes for arthritics.
Many arthritics experience more pain in cold weather.
New treatments give hope to arthritics worldwide.

senses.definition: Plural of arthritis: different types of joint inflammation diseases.
examples: Rheumatoid and psoriatic arthritides require long-term management.
The book covers the most common inflammatory arthritides.
These arthritides share similar symptoms but have different causes.

senses.definition: A condition that causes pain, swelling, and stiffness in one or more joints.
examples: Her grandmother has arthritis in both knees.
Exercise and medicine can help manage arthritis.
He was diagnosed with arthritis at a young age.

senses.definition: Plural of arthritis: different cases or types of arthritis.
examples: The clinic treats many forms of arthritises.
Researchers compare various arthritises to find common risk factors.
These arthritises present similar symptoms in early stages.

senses.definition: A tendency to develop arthritis or conditions related to arthritis.
examples: Arthritism may run in certain families.
The study explored genetic factors behind arthritism.
Early signs of arthritism can appear in young adults.

senses.definition: A type of rod-shaped bacteria in the genus Arthrobacter, often found in soil.
examples: Arthrobacterium species are common in agricultural soils.
The sample contained Arthrobacterium along with other microbes.
Researchers isolated an Arthrobacterium strain that degrades pollutants.

senses.definition: A gill attached to the joint of a limb in some crustaceans.
examples: The crab’s arthrobranch is located near the leg joint.
Scientists studied the structure of the arthrobranch under a microscope.
Damage to the arthrobranch can affect the animal’s breathing.

senses.definition: Gill structures attached to limb joints in certain crustaceans.
examples: The arthrobranchia play an important role in gas exchange.
Researchers compared the arthrobranchia of different crab species.
Damage to the arthrobranchia reduced the animal’s oxygen uptake.

senses.definition: A condition in which a joint becomes filled with pus and destroyed by infection.
examples: Arthrocace can lead to permanent loss of joint function.
The surgeon drained the arthrocace to control the infection.
Untreated arthrocace may require amputation.

senses.definition: A carcinoma that affects or involves a joint.
examples: Arthrocarcinoma is extremely rare in clinical practice.
The tumor was classified as an arthrocarcinoma invading the knee joint.
Radiographs revealed arthrocarcinoma with bone destruction.

senses.definition: A swelling or hernia of a joint.
examples: The physician detected an arthrocele near the elbow.
An untreated arthrocele may become painful over time.
Surgery was required to repair the arthrocele.

senses.definition: Inflammation of both a joint and the cartilage in it.
examples: Arthrochondritis can cause severe pain during movement.
X‑rays confirmed arthrochondritis in the patient’s hip.
Treatment for arthrochondritis often includes rest and anti-inflammatory drugs.

senses.definition: A surgical procedure in which a stiff joint is broken or forced to move to restore motion.
examples: Arthroclasia was considered to improve the range of motion.
The patient underwent arthroclasia of the elbow after the fracture healed.
Arthroclasia carries risks of further joint damage.

senses.definition: Surgical fusion or stiffening of a joint to stop it from moving.
examples: Arthrocleisis was chosen to stabilize the painful joint.
After arthrocleisis, the joint no longer allowed movement.
Severe deformities sometimes require arthrocleisis.

senses.definition: Another term for arthrocleisis, the surgical fusion or stiffening of a joint.
examples: Arthroclisis was performed to correct the severe foot deformity.
The surgeon explained that arthroclisis would permanently fix the joint.
In some cases, arthroclisis is the only way to relieve pain.

senses.definition: The outer covering or skin of a jointed limb or segment in some invertebrates.
examples: The arthroderm of the insect’s leg was carefully examined.
Damage to the arthroderm may limit movement.
The arthroderm thickens at each joint.

senses.definition: Plural of arthrodesis: surgical fusions of joints to stop movement and relieve pain.
examples: The surgeon has performed many arthrodeses on ankles and wrists.
Arthrodeses are considered when other treatments fail.
Outcomes of spinal arthrodeses were analyzed in the study.

senses.definition: Surgical fusion of a joint so that the bones grow together and no longer move.
examples: Arthrodesis can relieve pain in a severely damaged joint.
After ankle arthrodesis, the patient needed intensive physiotherapy.
The doctor explained the benefits and risks of arthrodesis.

senses.definition: A type of joint that allows only gliding movements between flat surfaces.
examples: The small bones of the wrist are connected by arthrodia.
Arthrodia provide limited but important movement.
In anatomy class, we studied arthrodia and other joint types.

senses.definition: Plural of arthrodia: joints that allow only gliding movements.
examples: The arthrodiae of the spine contribute to flexibility.
These arthrodiae can degenerate with age.
The study examined several arthrodiae in the wrist.

senses.definition: Relating to or having the nature of a gliding joint.
examples: Arthrodial joints allow only limited movement.
The wrist contains several arthrodial surfaces.
Degeneration of arthrodial cartilage can cause pain.

senses.definition: Having or relating to an arthrodial, gliding type of joint.
examples: The vertebrae are connected by arthrodic joints.
Arthrodic movement occurs between the small bones.
These arthrodic surfaces may become worn over time.

senses.definition: An extinct order of armored prehistoric fishes with jointed skull and neck plates.
examples: Arthrodira were dominant predators in Devonian seas.
Fossils of Arthrodira show heavy armor and powerful jaws.
The museum has a large display of Arthrodira specimens.

senses.definition: Relating to or belonging to the order Arthrodira of extinct armored fishes.
examples: Arthrodiran fossils have been found on several continents.
The arthrodiran skull shows a distinctive joint.
Researchers compared arthrodiran species from different regions.

senses.definition: A fish that belongs to the extinct armored order Arthrodira.
examples: The arthrodire had a heavily armored head and neck.
Paleontologists discovered a new arthrodire in the fossil beds.
This arthrodire is displayed in the museum’s Devonian gallery.

senses.definition: Having the characteristics of an arthrodire, especially jointed armored plates.
examples: The arthrodirous features of the skull are clearly visible.
Arthrodirous armor protected the head and shoulders.
These fossils show a strongly arthrodirous structure.

senses.definition: A taxonomic group or tribe historically used for certain joint-mouthed fish or animals.
examples: Arthrodonteae were once considered a separate group in early classifications.
The name Arthrodonteae appears in older zoological literature.
Modern systems rarely use Arthrodonteae as a formal category.
